commit.message:
feat: in-platform messaging system (channels, DMs, request threads)

Backend (messages.py):
- threads collection: channel/dm/request types with members list
- messages collection: thread_id linked, read_by tracking, sender info
- GET/POST /messages/threads: list + create threads
- GET /messages/threads/:id: single thread
- GET /messages/threads/by-reference/:id: find request thread by order_id
- GET/POST /messages/threads/:id/messages: paginated messages + send
- PATCH /messages/threads/:id/messages/:id/read: mark as read
- GET /messages/unread-count: total unread for current user
- Auto-marks messages as read on fetch
- Clients restricted to dm + request threads only
- create_thread_for_request() helper for auto-creating order threads
- Registered in __init__.py and server_v2.py

Frontend (Conversations.js):
- Two-column layout: thread sidebar (280px) + message view
- Channels section (admin/operator only) with + create button
- DMs section with + new message button and user picker
- Request threads section
- Message list with avatars, timestamps, auto-scroll
- Send bar: Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline
- 10-second polling for new messages
- Unread dot indicators on threads
- Empty states for no threads and no messages

Navigation:
- Route /conversations in App.js
- "Messages" nav item in both admin and client sidebars
- /conversations added to CLIENT_ALLOWED
- MessageSquare icon imported in Layout.js

* feat(SPF-1): hub lifecycle, room system, player identity, phonetic encoding

Implements the Server Platform Foundation (SPF-1) layer on top of
the existing ENet server:

New modules:
- phonetic.h/c: CV syllable encoding for IP:port. 8 syllables
  (16 consonants x 4 vowels = 6 bits each) encode 48 bits as a
  readable string like "BALE-GIFE-NOME-RIVA". Shorter alternative
  to the word-based connect codes for quick sharing.

- identity.h/c: pd-identity.dat persistence. Device UUID (16 bytes,
  generated once via xorshift128 + SDL perf counter entropy) plus
  up to 4 agent profiles (name, head, body). Survives across sessions.

- room.h/c: hub_room_t struct with 5-state lifecycle (LOBBY ->
  LOADING -> MATCH -> POSTGAME -> CLOSED). Max 4 rooms. Room 0 is
  permanent and wraps the existing single-match flow; backward
  compatible with all existing net/lobby code.

- hub.h/c: Singleton hub (LOUNGE / ACTIVE). Owns the room array.
  hubTick() syncs room 0 state from g_Lobby.inGame each frame,
  keeping hub state accurate without touching net.c.

Modified:
- server_main.c: hubInit() after lobbyInit(), hubTick() in frame
  loop, hubShutdown() in cleanup.
- server_gui.cpp: middle panel converted to tab bar. "Server" tab
  retains the existing player list + controls. "Hub" tab shows hub
  state and room list with color-coded lifecycle states. Log panel
  gains purple color for HUB: prefix lines.
- build-headless.ps1: pass explicit TEMP/TMP to child processes to
  work around sandbox environments where TEMP points to C:\Windows.

Protocol: v21 unchanged. No new messages. No constraint changes.

* Fix match start: SVC_STAGE_START sent STAGE_CITRAINING due to async mainChangeToStage()

netmsgSvcStageStartWrite() was writing g_StageNum directly, but the server
idles at STAGE_CITRAINING and mainChangeToStage() is deferred — g_StageNum
doesn't update until the next frame.  Result: the broadcast SVC_STAGE_START
always contained stagenum=0x26 (STAGE_CITRAINING), which clients interpreted
as "server returned to lobby" and ignored, so matches never started.

Fix: use g_MainChangeToStageNum (the pending stage) when it has been set
(>= 0), falling back to g_StageNum for mid-game joins after the stage
has loaded (at which point g_MainChangeToStageNum is reset to -1).

* Fix match start: stage 0x00 and bot spawn bugs in dedicated server

Two related bugs prevented matches from starting correctly:

1. Stage 0x00 sent to clients (server_stubs.c):
   mainChangeToStage stub didn't set g_MainChangeToStageNum.
   netmsgSvcStageStartWrite uses g_MainChangeToStageNum when >= 0,
   falling back to g_StageNum (0) when -1. The real game client sets
   g_MainChangeToStageNum; the stub now does the same.

2. Bots not spawning on client (netmsg.c):
   SVC_STAGE_START handler called mpStartMatch() without first
   populating g_MpParticipants from the received chrslots bitmask.
   mpStartMatch rebuilds participants for NETMODE_SERVER only, so
   the client pool was empty, mpHasSimulants() returned false, and
   bots never spawned. Fix: call mpParticipantsFromLegacyChrslots()
   before mpStartMatch() in the combat sim client path.
   Stub added to server_stubs.c (server never enters this path).

  netmsg.c CLC_LOBBY_START handler hardcoded g_MpSetup.timelimit=0 ("unlimited"
  comment was wrong: timelimit=0 means 1 minute; >=60 means unlimited).
  Fix: wire g_MatchConfig.timelimit through CLC_LOBBY_START payload.

B-41 (LOW): Spawn weapon not auto-equipping.
  g_MpSetup.options was hardcoded 0 in CLC_LOBBY_START handler, stripping
  MPOPTION_SPAWNWITHWEAPON. player.c already has equip logic gated on that bit.
  Fix: wire g_MatchConfig.options through CLC_LOBBY_START payload alongside
  timelimit. Server now applies both fields from the client's match config.

B-42: Add Bot button limited to 7 bots (MAX_PLAYERS - humans).
  pdgui_menu_room.cpp maxBots used MAX_PLAYERS=8 as ceiling, limiting bots to 7
  with 1 human. Game/server support up to 32. Fix: use MATCH_MAX_SLOTS cap.

Protocol: CLC_LOBBY_START gains 5 bytes (u8 timelimit + u32 options).
Callers updated: pdgui_bridge.c, pdgui_menu_room.cpp, netmenu.c.
netLobbyRequestStart (co-op/counter-op) defaults to timelimit=60, options=0.

fix: auto-reload stream after network loss (stall detection)

AVPlayer for live streams does not report .failed on network drop —
it enters .waitingToPlayAtSpecifiedRate indefinitely while isPlaying
stays true, leaving the UI in a broken state (silent audio, wrong
button icon). Added timeControlStatus KVO observer that triggers an
8s timeout and reloads the stream if still stalled, matching the
behavior fix from the existing radioAPARAT app.
